summary: I was born in a hospital for unwed mothers in Busan, South Korea, on May 23, 1994. Busan is where my first journey began, and it was from there that I flew to Saint Paul, Minnesota, when I was just five months old. Although I left Busan as a baby, it holds the place of my birth and the start of my story, connected to my birth family and foster mother, Mrs. Lee.

South Korea is where my life began, specifically in Busan, in 1994. My birth parents were young and working in a department store there, and after spending two months with my foster mother, Mrs. Lee, I was adopted and brought to the United States. South Korea represents my roots, the place of my birth and early care, even though I grew up far away from it in Minnesota.
cues: birth parents working at a department store
adoption and move to the US

summary: Saint Paul, Minnesota, is where my second journey began when I arrived from Busan at five months old on October 29, 1994. It’s where my adoptive family welcomed me, and where I grew up surrounded by love and kindness. My adoptive mother sang me lullabies and taught me how to live, and my dad’s warm hugs made me feel safe from the very first night. Saint Paul became my true home, the place where I found peace and happiness.
cues: first night sleeping on dad’s chest
